svg.text: How recurring community claims compare with the current repository’s code evidence

svg.text: Why it lands that way in this repo

g.font-family: Inter, PingFang SC, Microsoft YaHei, sans-serif
g.text: Claude Code’s moat is the composition
of tools, memory, commands, and agents
源码证实
The repo’s complexity is distributed across tools.ts, commands.ts,
loadSkillsDir.ts, mcp/client.ts, query.ts, and AppStateStore.ts.
Hooks are powerful, but lifecycle granularity
still limits advanced automation
部分成立
Hooks are deeply integrated, yet community issues still ask for
finer-grained events and better automation triggers.
MCP is the strongest extension surface and also
the most operationally brittle one
源码证实
The MCP client handles multiple transports, auth strategies,
timeouts, server caps, and session ingress edge cases.
Sub-agents raise throughput, but context, identity,
and session-stop semantics still feel rough
部分成立
The repo has real agent/task plumbing, but community feedback still
clusters around stop behavior, identity handoff, and observability.
CLAUDE.md and prompt files are productivity multipliers
and a live trust boundary at the same time
源码证实
Trust checks, memory loading, settings layering, and print-mode
exceptions all show that prompt files change runtime behavior.
Reverse engineering cannot stop at file counting;
you need runtime-gate awareness
源码证实
feature() => false, USER_TYPE guards, stubs, and dynamic activation
make “present in tree” very different from “active at runtime”.
